label: NetSuite
description: Connect to Oracle NetSuite ERP to read and write financial, inventory, and operational data using Token-Based Authentication.

form.title: Connect NetSuite
form.subtitle: Authenticate with Token-Based Authentication (TBA) to access your NetSuite account data.

methods.label: Token-Based Authentication (TBA)
methods.description: Paste your NetSuite Account ID, consumer key/secret, and token ID/secret. The recommended auth method for NetSuite integrations — no password sharing, scoped to a specific integration record.
methods.how_to:
## Set up Token-Based Authentication in NetSuite

TBA requires an Integration Record and a set of access tokens generated in your NetSuite account. You'll need Administrator or a role with Token Management permissions.

### 1. Enable Token-Based Authentication

1. Go to **Setup → Company → Enable Features**.
2. Click the **SuiteCloud** tab.
3. Under **Manage Authentication**, check **Token-Based Authentication**.
4. Save.

### 2. Create an Integration Record

1. Go to **Setup → Integration → Manage Integrations → New**.
2. Give it a name like `Anton`.
3. Under **Authentication**, check **Token-Based Authentication**. Uncheck **TBA: Authorization Flow** unless you need it.
4. Save. NetSuite shows a **Consumer Key** and **Consumer Secret** — copy both immediately. They are shown only once.

### 3. Create an Access Token

1. Go to **Setup → Users/Roles → Access Tokens → New**.
2. Select the Integration Record you just created.
3. Select the **User** and the **Role** Anton should operate as.
4. Save. NetSuite shows a **Token ID** and **Token Secret** — copy both immediately. Shown only once.

### 4. Find your Account ID

- Go to **Setup → Company → Company Information**. Your Account ID is listed there (e.g. `1234567` or `1234567_SB1` for sandboxes).

### 5. Paste everything below

Fill in all five fields. Anton stores them encrypted locally and uses OAuth 1.0 HMAC-SHA256 signing on every request.

### Notes
- Sandbox accounts use a suffix like `_SB1` in the Account ID — make sure to include it.
- If a token is compromised, revoke it under **Setup → Users/Roles → Access Tokens** and generate a new one.
- The role assigned to the token determines what data Anton can read or write — use the principle of least privilege.

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Found at Setup → Company → Company Information. Include the _SB1 suffix for sandbox accounts.

fields.name: consumer_key
fields.label: Consumer Key
fields.placeholder: Paste the consumer key from your Integration Record
fields.description: Generated when you saved the Integration Record. Shown only once — generate a new one if lost.

fields.name: consumer_secret
fields.label: Consumer Secret
fields.placeholder: Paste the consumer secret from your Integration Record
fields.description: Paired with the consumer key. Also shown only once when the Integration Record was saved.

fields.name: token_id
fields.label: Token ID
fields.placeholder: Paste the token ID from your Access Token
fields.description: Generated when you saved the Access Token under Setup → Users/Roles → Access Tokens.

fields.name: token_secret
fields.label: Token Secret
fields.placeholder: Paste the token secret from your Access Token
fields.description: Paired with the token ID. Shown only once at token creation time.
